items.title: Why Walmart’s Post-Earnings Dip Is A Diversification Play
items.summary: Walmart (NASDAQ: WMT) stock has dropped 11% over the past five trading days. This downturn comes right on the heels of the company's recent Q1 2027 earnings release, where, despite strong e-commerce growth and beating revenue forecasts, the stock faced immediate pressure. It is a pullback that should make investors take notice.

items.title: Jay Chaudhry, Zscaler CEO: Fortt Knox Earnings
items.summary: Zscaler CEO Jay Chaudhry told Jon Fortt the stock's ~30% post-earnings plunge is a buying opportunity, defending a Q3 that beat: revenue and ARR each up 25%, a record 23% operating margin, ARR near $3.5B. He called the softer guidance 'prudent' after two sales leaders departed, and said Zscaler is pulling memory and datacenter purchases forward in an AI-driven supply crunch to secure billions of coming AI agents – pressuring free cash flow.
